pool.title: Canopée
pool.why: Feathery tropical downtempo-house that sets the palm-shade mood without demanding the floor

pool.title: Estrelar
pool.why: Canonical Rio boogie — instant golden-hour authenticity and an easy tempo step up

pool.title: Djon Maya Maï (feat. Victor Démé)
pool.why: Sun-soaked organic house bridge that lifts tempo and emotion in one move

pool.title: Baianá
pool.why: Barbatuques chant over driving house — the moment the beach realizes it's a party

pool.title: Magalenha
pool.why: Carnival energy in club form; the major-key flip detonates the golden-hour peak

pool.title: Mwaki (feat. Sofiya Nzau)
pool.why: Brazilian producer, global anthem — sustains the peak with a deeper hypnotic pulse

pool.title: It Is What It Is (feat. Elise LeGrow)
pool.why: São Paulo's finest easing the throttle — melodic warmth as the sun touches the water

pool.title: Aganjú
pool.why: Bossa royalty over a gentle house pulse — the definitive Brazilian sunset landing

pool.title: Harvest Moon
pool.why: Daytime-disco cover that opens arms-wide and unhurried, perfect first-drink tempo

pool.title: Ponta de Lança Africano (Umbabarauma)
pool.why: Deathless samba-funk groove — crate-digger credibility that still moves hips

pool.title: Drinkee
pool.why: Portuguese-language jungle-pop house — playful tension that points at the peak

pool.title: Outro Lugar
pool.why: Bob Sinclar-era Brazilian house grail — pure uplift, pure Bahia

pool.title: In The Dark (with Fancy Inc)
pool.why: The Brazilian bass-house blueprint — locks the floor in at full commitment

pool.title: Hey Hey Hey (with Ashibah)
pool.why: Florianópolis peak-time chant weapon that keeps the crest rolling

pool.title: Sunshine (with LOthief & Santti)
pool.why: Literal sunshine energy at a softening intensity — the graceful step off the peak

pool.title: Samba de Janeiro
pool.why: Break-glass-in-case-of-carnival option if the crowd demands total eruption

pool.title: Move Your Body (with Sevek)
pool.why: Alternate Brazilian peak if the floor wants harder bass pressure

pool.title: Beautiful Life
pool.why: Brazilian melodic-tech euphoria for a dreamier peak substitution

pool.title: Lenda
pool.why: Sultry São Paulo dub-pop for after the last blend, feet in the sand

pool.title: Samba da Bênção
pool.why: Whispered bossa benediction — the encore that needs no beatmatch

pool.title: Baianá
pool.why: The original body-percussion source of the Bakermat edit — a storytelling interlude

pool.title: The Girl from Ipanema
pool.why: Pre-set ambience while the decks warm — the sound of Ipanema itself

pool.title: Me Dê Amor
pool.why: Baile-funk-flavored beat music for a mid-set texture swerve

pool.title: LK (Carolina Carol Bela) [feat. Stamina MC]
pool.why: Half-time trick card (174→87) — Brazilian liquid d&b for a jaw-drop moment if the night stretches

options.label: Copacabana Ascension
options.rationale: Organic golden-hour climb: tropical downtempo into Rio boogie, then percussive Brazilian house to a Magalenha/Mwaki double-peak, easing down through Vintage Culture into a bossa-house sunset closer. Every step is Camelot-adjacent (5A→6A→7A→8A→8B→9B→9A→10A) and no BPM jump exceeds 5.5%.

transitions.note: 105→110: let Estrelar's bassline surface under Canopée's outro pads, 5A→6A
110→116: ride Estrelar's percussion out, bring Djon Maya Maï in over 16 bars, 6A→7A
116→121: layer Baianá's chant over the Synapson breakdown, 7A→8A
121→123: swap during Baianá's drum break into Magalenha's whistle intro, 8A→8B relative-major lift
123→125: cut on the one out of Magalenha's final chorus into Mwaki's vocal loop, 8B→9B
125→122: hypnotic outro of Mwaki dissolves into It Is What It Is pads, 9B→9A
122→118: echo the last vocal, land Aganjú as the sun hits the horizon, 9A→10A

options.label: Bahia Bass Pressure
options.rationale: Same arc, heavier spine: daytime-disco and samba-funk warmup, Sofi Tukker tension, then a classic-to-modern Brazilian house corridor (Outro Lugar → In The Dark → Hey Hey Hey) before Sunshine and the shared Aganjú landing. Chain runs 1A→12A→11A→12A→12B→11B→10B→10A with max 5.2% BPM steps.

transitions.note: 108→112: Harvest Moon's shimmer into Umbabarauma's guitar vamp, 1A→12A
112→116: cut from Jorge Ben's chant straight into Drinkee's riff, 12A→11A
116→122: stretch Drinkee's outro, lift into Outro Lugar's strings, 11A→12A
122→124: swap in In The Dark during Outro Lugar's breakdown, 12A→12B energy flip
124→125: bass-house handoff, filter Hey Hey Hey's chant in over 8 bars, 12B→11B
125→122: soften into Sunshine's vocal as intensity steps down, 11B→10B
122→118: echo-out into Aganjú for the shared bossa-house sunset close, 10B→10A
